body:value: The data refers to details on household access to energy data for Census 2011. It shows primary energy sources for lighting in 2011 as 55.3 percent rural, 92.7 percent urban and 67.2 percent overall, as against 43.5 percent rural, 87.6 percent urban and 55.8 percent overall in 2001. The difference in NSSO and Census data is possibly due to differences in questionnaire.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Power and Energy
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Power and Energy
Non Renewable
title: Household Access To Energy Data

body:value:
National Sample Survey Organization (NSSO) conducts surveys on HOUSEHOLD CONSUMER EXPENDITURES ON a thin sample of households and a quinquennial survey on a large sample of households. Household consumer expenditure is the sum total of monetary values of all the items (i.e. goods and services) consumed by the household on domestic account during the reference period.
The programme of quinquennial surveys on consumer expenditure and employment & unemployment has been adopted by the National Sample Survey Office (NSSO) since 1972-73.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: National Sample Survey
field_ministry_department:name: Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation
field_sector:name: Others
Statistics
title: Household Consumer Expenditure: National Sample Survey

body:value: The data refers to the impact of CMDM on enrolment rates. It provides details of sample states on Increase in Enrolment (%age of Sample School), Increase in Enrolment Attributed to CMDM and Other Contributing Factors for Increase in Enrolment. The outcome is based on the sample survey undertaken by Programme Evaluation Organisation (PEO), Planning commission which covered 48 districts and 480 schools across 17 states of India for 'Performance Evaluation Report of Cooked Mid-Day Meal (CMDM), 2010'. The reference period for the study was 2000 to 2006.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Education
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Education
Elementary
title: Impact of CMDM on enrolment rates - Based on Sample Survey

body:value: The data refers to the impact of CMDM on fresh enrolments. Fresh enrolments are number of new enrolments in primary school. To calculate the fresh enrolments only those sample schools were selected which had maintained enrolment figures for all the years in the reference period (2000 to 2006). CMDM was introduced in different years in different states during the reference period. The outcome is based on the sample survey undertaken by Programme Evaluation Organisation (PEO), Planning commission which covered 48 districts and 480 schools across 17 states of India for 'Performance Evaluation Report of Cooked Mid-Day Meal (CMDM), 2010'. The reference period for the study was 2000 to 2006.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Education
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Education
Elementary
title: Impact of CMDM on Fresh Enrolments - Based on Sample Survey

body:value: The data refers to Impact of ICDS on Colostrums Feeding. Initiation of breast feeding within one hour of a child's birth is considered to contribute to the long term immunity of the child. The table records the impact of ICDS in popularising the practice and it is observed that ICDS has had a considerable impact.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Health
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Health and Family welfare
Health
title: Impact of ICDS on Colostrums Feeding

body:value: The data refers to the Impact of ICDS on enrolment of and discontinuation by children aged 7-14 years in primary and post-primary education. At the national level the impact on enrolment is positive, while that on discontinuation is negative.

body:value: The data refers to the Impact of ICDS on immunisation Status of Children (12-23 months). It captures the impact of ICDS on immunization at the national level. It is found that the ICDS programme on immunisation has more in case of measles.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Health
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Health and Family welfare
Health
title: Impact of ICDS on immunisation Status of Children (12-23 months)

body:value: The data refers to the Impact of ICDS on nutritional status of children aged 7 months-60 months. Parameter estimates with a positive signs indicate a positive impact, while those with negative signs imply a negative impact of ICDS in influencing the nutritional status of the children surveyed. At a national level, it is found that there has been some positive impact of ICDS on normal children and those suffering from Grade I malnutrition. However, ICDS could not improve the nutritional status of children suffering from malnutrition of higher degrees (Grades II to IV).
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Health
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Health and Family welfare
Health
title: Impact of ICDS on nutritional status of children aged 7 months-60 months

body:value: The data refers to the Impact of Integrated Child Development Services (ICDS) on the practice of a child getting weighed at birth. Weighing of the child at birth is considered an important component of ICDS and The data refers to the estimated impact of ICDS on this practice.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Health
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Health and Family welfare
Health
title: Impact of ICDS on the practice of a child getting weighed at birth

body:value: The data refers to information on general impact of rural roads on the habitation after rural roads. It provides details of responses of beneficiaries regarding parameters such as economic well being, number of vehicles (bicycle, rickshaw, motorized vehicle), enrolment of children, employment opportunities, patients seeking treatment, immunization facilities and others for selected sample states. The outcome is based on the sample survey undertaken by Programme Evaluation Organisation (PEO), Planning commission which covered 14 districts, 27 blocks, 138 roads, 138 habitations and 1380 beneficiary households spread over 7 states of India for 'Evaluation Study on Rural Roads component of Bharat Nirman, 2010'. The reference period for the study was 2005-06 to 2006-07.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Rural Roads
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Infrastructure
Roads
Urban
Rural
Development
title: Impact of Rural Roads on the Habitation After Rural Roads - Based on Sample Survey

The outcome is based on the sample survey undertaken by Programme Evaluation Organisation (PEO), Planning commission which covered 14 districts, 27 blocks, 138 roads, 138 habitations and 1380 beneficiary households spread over 7 states of India for 'Evaluation Study on Rural Roads component of Bharat Nirman, 2010'. body:value: The data refers to details on Import Export Classification, ITC (HS) Code and Import Policy. ITC (HS) codes are better known as Indian Trade Clarification (ITC) and are based on Harmonized System (HS) of Coding. It was adopted in India for import-export operations. Indian custom uses an eight digit ITC (HS) code to suit the national trade requirements.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Import Export
field_ministry_department:name: Ministry of Commerce and Industry
Department of Commerce
field_sector:name: Commerce
Export
Import
title: Import Export Classification, ITC (HS) Code And Import Policy

body:value: Between 2001 and 2011, the overall literacy levels rose from 64.8 per cent to 74 per cent. Female literacy rates increased at a faster rate (11.79 per cent) than that for males (6.88 per cent), thus reducing gender gap in literacy from 21.59 to 16.68 percentage points.

Elementary
